title: cornus foemina subsp. microcarpa
intro: Cornus foemina subsp. microcarpa, also known as the small-fruited flowering dogwood, is a plant species in the dogwood family that is native to the southeastern United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that have been traditionally used to treat wounds, fevers, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cornus foemina subsp. microcarpa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
